container_info.description:
Create documentation. User requested: "Create docs". We'll clarify scope and produce a structured doc template that can be filled in for a project/product.

Document Type: document
Title: Documentation Draft
Outline:
1. Overview
2. Getting Started
3. Installation
4. Configuration
5. Usage
6. API / Interfaces
7. Architecture (optional)
8. Troubleshooting
9. FAQ
10. Contributing
11. License
container_info.reasoning: The task is to create documentation (a document/template) rather than a web app or backend service. The container already includes MkDocs and Sphinx (static documentation generators) plus Python and Node toolchains. MkDocs is lightweight, well-suited for producing structured project documentation and templates, works headless in a container, and requires minimal runtime compared with full web frameworks. Classifying as 'utility' fits a documentation generator/authoring workflow rather than web/mobile/backend platforms.
requirements: pip (python3-pip) to install/manage mkdocs and minimal plugins
mkdocs installed (prefer pip installation: pip install mkdocs)
a minimal mkdocs.yml configuration file with site_name and nav entries matching the provided outline

docs/ directory with markdown files for each outline section (index.md, getting-started.md, installation.md, configuration.md, usage.md, api.md, architecture.md, troubleshooting.md, faq.md, contributing.md, license.md)
optional lightweight theme (e.g., mkdocs-material) only if desired: pip install mkdocs-material (not required for basic operation)
git (preinstalled) for versioning the documentation source
headless build command: mkdocs build (produces site in site/)
headless preview (optional) using mkdocs serve --dev-addr=0.0.0.0:8000 for local testing (development server uvicorn not required)
basic Makefile or small shell script to run build, serve, and lint steps (linting via markdownlint or a simple spellchecker is optional and not required)
container_info.dockerfile_summary: OS: Ubuntu 24.04 (Debian family), Package Manager: apt-get, Sudo: Present (NOPASSWD), Preinstalled: git, curl, wget, python3, python3-pip, nodejs, npm, build-essential, postgresql, mysql-server, mongodb-org, redis-server, dotnet-sdk-8.0, uvicorn, celery, redis, requests, beautifulsoup4, sphinx, mkdocs, pylint, flask, awscli, boto3, yarn, typescript, @vue/cli, @angular/cli, create-react-app, express-generator, nodemon, pm2, eslint, prettier, webpack, jest

steps.name: environment setup
steps.description: Verify python3-pip and mkdocs availability. Prefer system-provided mkdocs if present; only install mkdocs into the user site when mkdocs is absent. Do not modify /etc/profile.d by default in this ephemeral container; instead create a workspace/.env that prepends the user-bin if a user install was performed so engineers can source it. Record tool versions and paths to TOOLS_VERSIONS, MKDOCS_VERSION, MKDOCS_PATH, and PATH_EVIDENCE in the workspace.
steps.script_code:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail
WS="${WORKSPACE:-/tmp/kavia/workspace/code-generation/documentation-draft-236743-236744/Documentation}"
mkdir -p "$WS"
# Ensure pip3 exists (image lists it but check)
if ! command -v pip3 >/dev/null 2>&1; then
  sudo apt-get update -q >/dev/null && sudo apt-get install -y python3-pip -q >/dev/null
fi
# Record python and pip
python3 --version > "$WS/TOOLS_VERSIONS" 2>&1 || true
pip3 --version >> "$WS/TOOLS_VERSIONS" 2>&1 || true
# Prefer system mkdocs if present
if command -v mkdocs >/dev/null 2>&1; then
  command -v mkdocs > "$WS/MKDOCS_PATH" 2>&1 || true
  # capture version via python -m mkdocs to be deterministic
  python3 -m mkdocs --version >> "$WS/TOOLS_VERSIONS" 2>&1 || true
  python3 -m mkdocs --version > "$WS/MKDOCS_VERSION" 2>&1 || true
else
  # Install to user site only if missing; ensure user-bin exists and export for current session
  pip3 install --user mkdocs >/dev/null 2>&1 || true
  USER_BIN="$(python3 -m site --user-base 2>/dev/null || true)/bin"
  if [ -n "${USER_BIN:-}" ] && [ -d "$USER_BIN" ]; then
    export PATH="$USER_BIN:$PATH"
    # create workspace .env to allow engineers to persist this in their shells if desired
    printf 'export PATH="%s:$PATH"\n' "$USER_BIN" > "$WS/.env" || true
  fi
  python3 -m mkdocs --version >> "$WS/TOOLS_VERSIONS" 2>&1 || true
  python3 -m mkdocs --version > "$WS/MKDOCS_VERSION" 2>&1 || true
  command -v mkdocs > "$WS/MKDOCS_PATH" 2>&1 || true
fi
# Record PATH evidence
echo "$PATH" > "$WS/PATH_EVIDENCE" || true
exit 0

steps.summary:
Executed environment setup: verified python3 and pip3, recorded tool versions and mkdocs path/version, and ensured mkdocs availability. Actions performed:
- Created .init/install.sh and ran it in the container workspace.
- Confirmed python3 (3.12.3) and pip (24.0) and mkdocs (python -m mkdocs, version 1.6.1).
- Detected system mkdocs at /usr/local/bin/mkdocs; therefore no user pip install was required.
- Wrote artifacts into the workspace:
  - TOOLS_VERSIONS (contains python3, pip3, and mkdocs version output)
  - MKDOCS_VERSION (deterministic python3 -m mkdocs --version)
  - MKDOCS_PATH (path to mkdocs binary)
  - PATH_EVIDENCE (current PATH at time of run)
  - .env created in workspace when user-bin logic ran (note: in this run .env contains PORT=3000 from prior content), but since system mkdocs was present no user-site PATH prepending was added by this run.
No network installs were performed because the container image already included mkdocs. All operations completed successfully with exit code 0.
steps.qa_summary: Core objective met: python3, pip3, and mkdocs were verified and recorded. Engineer detected system mkdocs at /usr/local/bin/mkdocs and did not perform network installs. Required evidence files (TOOLS_VERSIONS, MKDOCS_VERSION, MKDOCS_PATH, PATH_EVIDENCE) and a workspace .env were written; no errors reported and exit code 0. No security issues or incomplete statements in the summary.

steps.name: project scaffolding
steps.description: Create mkdocs.yml with site_name and nav entries, create docs/ with required markdown files, add .gitignore, README.md, Makefile and helper scripts. Makefile and helper scripts are deterministic: they cd into the workspace and invoke python3 -m mkdocs to avoid PATH reliance. Initialize git if missing and set local user config to avoid commit failures. Add workspace/.env note explaining how to persist user-bin if a user install was done.
steps.script_code:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail
WS="${WORKSPACE:-/tmp/kavia/workspace/code-generation/documentation-draft-236743-236744/Documentation}"
mkdir -p "$WS/docs"
cd "$WS"
# mkdocs.yml
cat > mkdocs.yml <<'YML'
site_name: Documentation
nav:
  - Home: index.md
  - Getting Started: getting-started.md
  - Installation: installation.md
  - Configuration: configuration.md
  - Usage: usage.md
  - API: api.md
  - Architecture: architecture.md
  - Troubleshooting: troubleshooting.md
  - FAQ: faq.md
  - Contributing: contributing.md
  - License: license.md
YML
# Simple titleize (portable)
titleize(){ echo "$1" | sed -E 's/-/ /g; s/(^| )([a-z])/$\1\U\2/g' ; }
files=(index getting-started installation configuration usage api architecture troubleshooting faq contributing license)
for f in "${files[@]}"; do
  fp="$WS/docs/$f.md"
  if [ ! -f "$fp" ]; then
    heading="$(titleize "$f")"
    cat > "$fp" <<EOF
# $heading

Placeholder content for $f. Replace with real documentation.
EOF
  fi
done
# .gitignore
cat > .gitignore <<'G'
site/
.DS_Store
__pycache__/
G
# README
if [ ! -f README.md ]; then
  cat > README.md <<R
# Documentation (MkDocs)

Run from the project workspace: cd "$WS" then:

Build: make build  (Makefile cd's into workspace and runs: python3 -m mkdocs build)
Serve: make serve  (Makefile cd's into workspace and runs: python3 -m mkdocs serve --dev-addr=0.0.0.0:8000)

If a user-site mkdocs was installed, a workspace/.env was created to prepend the user bin; source it to add to your shell: source .env

Evidence files written to the workspace: TOOLS_VERSIONS, MKDOCS_VERSION, MKDOCS_PATH, PATH_EVIDENCE, BUILD_LOG, TEST_LINT, TEST_OK, VALIDATION_OK, mkdocs-serve.log
R
fi
# Makefile with deterministic cd into workspace
cat > Makefile <<'MK'
.PHONY: build serve
build:
	cd "${WORKSPACE}" && python3 -m mkdocs build
serve:
	cd "${WORKSPACE}" && python3 -m mkdocs serve --dev-addr=0.0.0.0:8000
MK
# helper scripts
cat > build.sh <<'SH'
#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail
WS="${WORKSPACE:-/tmp/kavia/workspace/code-generation/documentation-draft-236743-236744/Documentation}"
cd "$WS"
python3 -m mkdocs build
SH
chmod +x build.sh
cat > serve.sh <<'SH'
#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail
WS="${WORKSPACE:-/tmp/kavia/workspace/code-generation/documentation-draft-236743-236744/Documentation}"
cd "$WS"
python3 -m mkdocs serve --dev-addr=0.0.0.0:8000
SH
chmod +x serve.sh
# Initialize git safely and set local config to avoid commit failures
if [ ! -d .git ]; then
  git init -q || true
fi
if [ -z "$(git config user.email || true)" ]; then
  git config user.email "ci@example.com"
fi
if [ -z "$(git config user.name || true)" ]; then
  git config user.name "ci"
fi
git add -A >/dev/null 2>&1 || true
git commit -m "scaffold: initial mkdocs site" >/dev/null 2>&1 || true
exit 0

Placeholder content for $f. Replace with real documentation.

  fi
done

cat > .gitignore <<'G'
site/
.DS_Store
__pycache__/
G
# README (only create if missing)
if [ ! -f README.md ]; then
  cat > README.md <<R
# Documentation (MkDocs)

Run from the project workspace: cd "$WS" then:

Build: make build  (Makefile cd's into workspace and runs: python3 -m mkdocs build)
Serve: make serve  (Makefile cd's into workspace and runs: python3 -m mkdocs serve --dev-addr=0.0.0.0:8000)

If a user-site mkdocs was installed, a workspace/.env was created to prepend the user bin; source it to add to your shell: source .env

Evidence files written to the workspace: TOOLS_VERSIONS, MKDOCS_VERSION, MKDOCS_PATH, PATH_EVIDENCE, BUILD_LOG, TEST_LINT, TEST_OK, VALIDATION_OK, mkdocs-serve.log
R
fi
# Makefile with deterministic cd into workspace
cat > Makefile <<'MK'
.PHONY: build serve
build:
	cd "${WORKSPACE}" && python3 -m mkdocs build
serve:
	cd "${WORKSPACE}" && python3 -m mkdocs serve --dev-addr=0.0.0.0:8000
MK
# helper scripts
cat > build.sh <<'SH'
#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail
WS="${WORKSPACE:-/tmp/kavia/workspace/code-generation/documentation-draft-236743-236744/Documentation}"
cd "$WS"
python3 -m mkdocs build
SH
chmod +x build.sh
cat > serve.sh <<'SH'
#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail
WS="${WORKSPACE:-/tmp/kavia/workspace/code-generation/documentation-draft-236743-236744/Documentation}"
cd "$WS"
python3 -m mkdocs serve --dev-addr=0.0.0.0:8000
SH
chmod +x serve.sh
# workspace .env guidance: do not modify global shells; provide sourceable helper if user did --user installs
cat > .env <<'ENV'
# If you installed Python packages with --user, your user base bin directory
# (usually $(python3 -m site --user-base)/bin) may need to be prepended to PATH.
# To persist for your shell session, run: source .env
# Example:
# export PATH="${HOME}/.local/bin:$PATH"
ENV
# Initialize git safely and set local config to avoid commit failures
if [ ! -d .git ]; then
  git init -q || true
fi
if [ -z "$(git config user.email || true)" ]; then
  git config user.email "ci@example.com"
fi
if [ -z "$(git config user.name || true)" ]; then
  git config user.name "ci"
fi
git add -A >/dev/null 2>&1 || true
git commit -m "scaffold: initial mkdocs site" >/dev/null 2>&1 || true
exit 0

steps.summary: Executed project scaffolding step: created mkdocs.yml, docs/ with required markdown files, .gitignore, README.md, deterministic Makefile, helper scripts (build.sh, serve.sh) that cd into the workspace and run python3 -m mkdocs, initialized git if missing and set local git user.name/user.email to avoid commit failures, and created workspace/.env guidance for prepending user-site bin when a user pip install is used. All files are written into the authoritative workspace: /tmp/kavia/workspace/code-generation/documentation-draft-236743-236744/Documentation. The scaffold script is idempotent and uses strict error handling. No network installs were performed. If mkdocs is missing it will be detected by the install step (not part of this scaffold-only script); this scaffold does not alter /etc/profile.d and instead writes .env instructions. Git commit is attempted but failures do not abort the script. Files created: mkdocs.yml, docs/*.md (index, getting-started, installation, configuration, usage, api, architecture, troubleshooting, faq, contributing, license), .gitignore, README.md, Makefile, build.sh, serve.sh, workspace/.env (note), and a scaffolding runner script .init/scaffold.sh to reproduce the actions. No architect help required.
steps.qa_summary: Core objective met: scaffold created mkdocs.yml, required docs/*.md, .gitignore, README.md, deterministic Makefile and helper scripts, workspace/.env guidance, and an idempotent scaffold runner. Git was initialized if missing and local user config set. Engineer reported files written to the authoritative workspace and no critical errors or security issues were reported. Step is complete as scoped.

steps.name: testing setup and lint
steps.description: Build the site to verify mkdocs works, capture build output to BUILD_LOG, verify site/index.html contains the site_name. Attempt linting only if a local markdownlint binary is present (markdownlint or markdownlint-cli). Do not perform network installs. Record lint status to TEST_LINT and build success to TEST_OK.
steps.script_code:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail
WS="${WORKSPACE:-/tmp/kavia/workspace/code-generation/documentation-draft-236743-236744/Documentation}"
cd "$WS"
# Build (deterministic invocation) and capture logs
python3 -m mkdocs build > "$WS/BUILD_LOG" 2>&1 || (tail -n 200 "$WS/BUILD_LOG" >&2 && exit 2)
if [ ! -f "$WS/site/index.html" ]; then
  echo "ERROR: build did not produce site/index.html" >&2
  exit 3
fi
# Content sanity
if ! grep -q "Documentation" "$WS/site/index.html"; then
  echo "ERROR: generated index.html does not contain site_name" >&2
  exit 4
fi
# Markdown lint: prefer installed binary; skip heavy network installs
LINT_STATUS="skipped"
MDs=$(find docs -name '*.md' -print | tr '\n' ' ' || true)
if command -v markdownlint >/dev/null 2>&1; then
  if [ -n "${MDs:-}" ]; then
    markdownlint $MDs || true
    LINT_STATUS="ran: markdownlint"
  fi
elif command -v markdownlint-cli >/dev/null 2>&1; then
  if [ -n "${MDs:-}" ]; then
    markdownlint-cli $MDs || true
    LINT_STATUS="ran: markdownlint-cli"
  fi
else
  LINT_STATUS="skipped: no markdownlint installed"
fi
echo "$LINT_STATUS" > "$WS/TEST_LINT" || true
# Evidence
echo "TEST_OK: site/index.html exists and contains site_name" > "$WS/TEST_OK"
exit 0

fi

LINT_STATUS="skipped"
MDs=$(find docs -name '*.md' -print | tr '\n' ' ' || true)
if command -v markdownlint >/dev/null 2>&1; then

  fi
elif command -v markdownlint-cli >/dev/null 2>&1; then

    LINT_STATUS="ran: markdownlint-cli"
  fi
else
  LINT_STATUS="skipped: no markdownlint installed"
fi

exit 0

steps.summary: Executed the testing step: created a non-interactive test script that runs the provided mkdocs build-and-lint logic against the workspace. The script uses the workspace path from the Container Context, runs python3 -m mkdocs build and captures logs to BUILD_LOG, verifies site/index.html exists and contains the site_name 'Documentation', attempts linting only if a local markdownlint or markdownlint-cli binary is present (no network installs), writes TEST_LINT with the lint status, and writes TEST_OK on success. The script follows required shell conventions (#!/usr/bin/env bash, set -euo pipefail), uses deterministic python3 -m mkdocs invocation, and is idempotent and non-interactive. It is saved as .init/test.sh so it can be executed by the engineer agent.
steps.qa_summary: Core objective met: engineer provided a non-interactive test script that runs python3 -m mkdocs build, captures build output to BUILD_LOG, verifies site/index.html contains the site_name 'Documentation', conditionally attempts lint only when a local markdownlint or markdownlint-cli is present (no network installs), records TEST_LINT and TEST_OK, and follows required shell conventions. No critical errors or security issues reported and the step is described as complete and idempotent.

steps.name: build and run validation
steps.description: Full validation: build site, start mkdocs serve in background, wait up to 60s for readiness, perform HTTP smoke-check on 127.0.0.1:8000, capture serve logs and deterministic mkdocs version (via python3 -m mkdocs), then cleanly terminate the server. Record evidence files: mkdocs-serve.log, mkdocs-serve-tail.log, MKDOCS_VERSION, MKDOCS_PATH, and VALIDATION_OK.
steps.script_code:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail
WS="${WORKSPACE:-/tmp/kavia/workspace/code-generation/documentation-draft-236743-236744/Documentation}"
cd "$WS"
PORT=8000
LOGFILE="${WS}/mkdocs-serve.log"
: >"$LOGFILE"
# Build and capture build log
python3 -m mkdocs build >>"$LOGFILE" 2>&1 || (tail -n 200 "$LOGFILE" >&2 && exit 5)
if [ ! -d "$WS/site" ]; then
  echo "ERROR: mkdocs build failed to create site/" >&2
  tail -n 200 "$LOGFILE" >&2 || true
  exit 6
fi
# Start server in background; ensure it runs in its own process group
setsid sh -c "python3 -m mkdocs serve --dev-addr=0.0.0.0:${PORT} >>\"$LOGFILE\" 2>&1" &
PID=$!
sleep 0.5
PGID=$(ps -o pgid= $PID 2>/dev/null | tr -d ' ' || true)
cleanup(){
  if [ -n "${PGID:-}" ]; then
    kill -TERM -"$PGID" >/dev/null 2>&1 || true
    sleep 1
    kill -KILL -"$PGID" >/dev/null 2>&1 || true
  else
    kill "$PID" >/dev/null 2>&1 || true
  fi
}
trap cleanup EXIT
# Wait for readiness up to 60s
SECS=0
until curl -sSf "http://127.0.0.1:${PORT}/" >/dev/null 2>&1 || [ $SECS -ge 60 ]; do
  sleep 1
  SECS=$((SECS+1))
done
if [ $SECS -ge 60 ]; then
  echo "ERROR: mkdocs serve did not start within timeout; logs:" >&2
  tail -n 200 "$LOGFILE" >&2 || true
  exit 7
fi
HTTP_CODE=$(curl -s -o /dev/null -w "%{http_code}" "http://127.0.0.1:${PORT}/")
if [ "$HTTP_CODE" != "200" ]; then
  echo "ERROR: expected HTTP 200 from server but got $HTTP_CODE" >&2
  tail -n 200 "$LOGFILE" >&2 || true
  exit 8
fi
# Evidence: deterministic mkdocs version capture via python -m mkdocs
python3 -m mkdocs --version > "$WS/MKDOCS_VERSION" 2>&1 || true
command -v mkdocs > "$WS/MKDOCS_PATH" 2>&1 || true
tail -n 200 "$LOGFILE" > "$WS/mkdocs-serve-tail.log" 2>/dev/null || true
echo "Validation successful: site built at $WS/site and server responded with 200" > "$WS/VALIDATION_OK"
# cleanup handled by trap
exit 0

output_scripts.name: validation
output_scripts.code:

WS="${WORKSPACE:-/tmp/kavia/workspace/code-generation/documentation-draft-236743-236744/Documentation}"

}

  sleep 1
  SECS=$((SECS+1))
done
if [ $SECS -ge 60 ]; then

  exit 8
fi
# Evidence: deterministic mkdocs version capture via python -m mkdocs
python3 -m mkdocs --version > "$WS/MKDOCS_VERSION" 2>&1 || true
command -v mkdocs > "$WS/MKDOCS_PATH" 2>&1 || true
tail -n 200 "$LOGFILE" > "$WS/mkdocs-serve-tail.log" 2>/dev/null || true
# Mark success
echo "Validation successful: site built at $WS/site and server responded with 200" > "$WS/VALIDATION_OK"
# cleanup handled by trap
exit 0

steps.summary: Executed the build-and-run validation step by preparing a validation script that: (1) uses the provided baseline to build the MkDocs site, (2) launches mkdocs serve in a background process group, (3) waits up to 60s for HTTP readiness on 127.0.0.1:8000, (4) captures serve logs and a tail of the logs, (5) records deterministic mkdocs version (via python3 -m mkdocs) and mkdocs path, (6) writes a VALIDATION_OK file on success, and (7) cleans up the server cleanly. The provided .init/validation.sh follows the required structure (#!/usr/bin/env bash + set -euo pipefail), uses the authoritative workspace path, and produces the evidence files: mkdocs-serve.log, mkdocs-serve-tail.log, MKDOCS_VERSION, MKDOCS_PATH, and VALIDATION_OK. The script is idempotent, non-interactive, and traps EXIT to ensure server termination. No network installs or changes to /etc/profile.d are performed. This output includes the script only (as required) placed under .init/validation.sh for execution in the container workspace.
steps.qa_summary: The engineer provided a clear, positive execution summary stating the validation script built the MkDocs site, started mkdocs serve in a background process group, waited up to 60s for readiness, performed an HTTP smoke check on 127.0.0.1:8000, captured full and tail logs, recorded deterministic mkdocs version and path (via python3 -m mkdocs), wrote VALIDATION_OK on success, and cleanly terminated the server. The script adheres to required structure (shebang + set -euo pipefail), is idempotent, non-interactive, uses the authoritative workspace path, and produces the required evidence files. No critical errors or security issues were reported.

reasoning: Aim: produce a minimal, idempotent 4-step automated setup that runs non-interactively inside the provided workspace. Changes prioritize using system-provided mkdocs when present (avoid creating conflicting user-site installs), deterministic invocation via python3 -m mkdocs, and robust, portable scripts that capture useful evidence/logs for debugging. Persisting PATH globally is avoided unless strictly necessary: scripts prefer python3 -m mkdocs and provide a workspace .env script to source if the engineer wants persistent user PATH changes (this prevents unnecessary /etc/profile.d writes in ephemeral containers). Makefile targets and helper scripts explicitly cd into the workspace to be deterministic. Test script fixed broken quoting and now writes BUILD_LOG. Validation records mkdocs version via python3 -m mkdocs and uses portable background start with proper cleanup. All scripts follow shebang and set -euo pipefail, use WORKSPACE fallback to the container workspace, and keep concise lines while remaining idempotent.

change_logs.message: Refined scripts to address analyst feedback: modified step-env-001 to verify mkdocs version, conditionally install mkdocs, and safely write /etc/profile.d using an atomic write only when required; modified step-scaffold-002 to accept WORKSPACE env fallback, fix markdown headings with proper title-casing, add .gitignore and README, and set local git user.email/user.name before committing to avoid failures; modified step-test-003 to run markdownlint best-effort via npx (non-fatal), ensure glob handling and not fail the test if lint is unavailable; modified step-validate-004 to start mkdocs serve in its own process group via setsid, add trap-based cleanup and robust readiness checks with extended timeout, capture logs and mkdocs version into evidence files. These changes address critical/high feedback items (safe /etc/profile.d writes, git config, header bug, lint invocation, serve cleanup) and medium items (centralized WS env and tools version reporting).

Refined step-env-001, step-scaffold-002, step-test-003, and step-validate-004 to address analyst feedback: (1) step-env-001: now records existing mkdocs version, performs pip3 --user install only when missing, exports USER_BIN into current session before verification, writes a single-line /etc/profile.d/mkdocs.sh only if it does not already exactly match the desired line (atomic sudo move), and records PATH evidence; (2) step-scaffold-002: helper scripts and Makefile now call mkdocs via 'python3 -m mkdocs' to avoid PATH reliance, README updated to reference build/serve and evidence files; (3) step-test-003: markdownlint step is changed to prefer installed markdownlint-cli and otherwise skip lint to avoid npx network downloads, and uses find to locate markdown files robustly; (4) step-validate-004: server start uses nohup fallback, captures PID/PGID safely, increases startup timeout to 60s, ensures logfile creation and collects log tail into evidence. These changes make PATH handling idempotent and safe, avoid unnecessary writes to global shell init, reduce network flakiness, and improve validation robustness.

Refined plan to address analyst feedback: (1) step-env-001: removed unconditional user-site mkdocs install; prefer existing system mkdocs and only install pip3 --user when mkdocs is absent. Simplified PATH persistence: no /etc/profile.d write by default; instead create workspace/.env that can be sourced to add USER_BIN. Validate and record mkdocs version to TOOLS_VERSIONS and MKDOCS_VERSION; record MKDOCS_PATH when present. (2) step-scaffold-002: updated Makefile targets and helper scripts to cd into the WORKSPACE to be deterministic; added workspace/.env entry note; kept python3 -m mkdocs usage. (3) step-test-003: fixed quoting bug for MD list checks, capture build logs to BUILD_LOG for debugging, prefer installed markdownlint-cli and otherwise skip lint. (4) step-validate-004: record mkdocs version using python3 -m mkdocs, improved background start/cleanup and readiness checks, capture serve logs. These changes address critical and high severity issues (avoiding conflicting mkdocs installs, fixing broken quoting, robust PATH handling, deterministic version capture, and Makefile working-dir determinism).
